questions: Within the group of occupations identified as similar to software developers, quality assurance analysts, and testers, find the occupation that is projected to grow 15 percent from 2024 to 2034 and typically requires 5 years or more of related work experience for entry. For this occupation, provide its 2024 median annual pay, projected average annual job openings over the decade, and typical entry-level education. Then, identify the academic field-of-degree category that aligns with this occupation’s typical educational background, and list at least four other majors included in that same field-of-degree listing, along with the page’s last modified date.

rounds.rationale: The question uses logical filters (similar occupation, 15% growth rate, and 5+ years of related work experience) to indirectly identify Computer and Information Systems Managers without naming the role (Deep reasoning). It then requires aggregation of multiple data points from that occupation’s profile and cross-referencing with the relevant academic field-of-degree directory, including additional majors and a page date (Wide information gathering).

rounds.answer_a:
The provided search results are insufficient to fully answer this query. While the search results confirm that **software developers, quality assurance analysts, and testers are projected to grow 15 percent from 2024 to 2034** with a 2024 median annual pay of approximately **$131,450** and **129,200 average annual job openings** [1][2], the results lack critical information required to complete your request.

Specifically, the search results do not contain:

- Details about entry-level experience requirements (5+ years vs. other thresholds) for software development occupations or related roles
- Comprehensive information about typical entry-level education requirements for these positions
- Academic field-of-degree category classifications
- Lists of related majors within a specific field-of-degree category
- Last modified dates for occupational profile pages

To properly answer this query, I would need access to the full Bureau of Labor Statistics occupational profile pages for software developers and related occupations, which typically include sections on entry requirements and educational pathways. The current results provide only high-level employment projections and salary data.

I recommend visiting the BLS Occupational Outlook Handbook directly or consulting detailed occupational profile pages that contain the specific entry requirements, education classifications, and related field information your query requires.

## References

[1] Bureau of Labor Statistics. Software Developers, Quality Assurance Analysts, and Testers. https://www.bls.gov/ooh/computer-and-information-technology/software-developers.htm

[2] NCHStats. US Software Jobs Are Set to Grow 15 Percent by 2034. https://nchstats.com/us-software-jobs-growth/
rounds.answer_b:
The occupation that aligns with software developers, quality assurance analysts, and testers, with a projected growth of 15 percent from 2024 to 2034 and requiring 5 years or more of related work experience for entry, is Computer and Information Systems Managers [1]. In 2024, the median annual pay for this occupation was $164,070 [1]. The projected average annual job openings over the 2024–2034 decade are estimated at 52,000 [1]. The typical entry-level education for this role is a bachelor’s degree, though many employers prefer candidates with a master’s in business administration (MBA) or a related field [1]. The academic field-of-degree category that aligns with this occupation is “Computer and Information Sciences and Support Services” [2]. Within this category, other common majors include Computer Science, Information Technology, Computer Systems Networking, and Cybersecurity [2]. These majors provide foundational knowledge in systems analysis, software development, and organizational technology strategy, which are crucial for advancement into management roles. This field-of-degree listing ensures graduates are well-prepared for both technical and leadership responsibilities in IT environments [2]. The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics page for Computer and Information Systems Managers was last modified on April 9, 2025 [1].

## References  
[1] U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics – Computer and Information Systems Managers, https://www.bls.gov/oes/current/oes113021.htm (last modified April 9, 2025)  
[2] U.S. Department of Education – Classification of Instructional Programs (CIP), "Computer and Information Sciences and Support Services", https://nces.ed.gov/ipeds/CIPCODE/cip_2020_detail.aspx?y=2020&c=11 (last accessed from NCES, 2025)
rounds.judge_reasoning:
First, Deep Logic: Agent B correctly identifies **Computer and Information Systems Managers** as the occupation similar to software developers and testers, matching 15% projected growth and 5+ years of related work experience. Agent A fails to identify any occupation and refuses to answer. 

Second, Width/Completeness: Agent B provides the required median pay, projected annual openings, entry-level education, field-of-degree category, four related majors, and a last modified date. While there is a minor issue (the last modified date appears tied to the BLS page rather than clearly to the field-of-degree page), the response substantially fulfills the checklist. Agent A provides none of the required elements.

Finally, User Experience: Agent B delivers a direct, structured, content-rich answer that resolves the query. Agent A provides a refusal with minimal utility. Therefore, Agent B is decisively superior on accuracy, completeness, and helpfulness.
